Article.ArticleTitle: Heterogeneous activation of persulfate by PDA coated Fe@MXene for efficient degradation of dyeing wastewater.

Abstract.AbstractText: Advanced oxidation processes (AOPs) based on transition metal-activated persulfate offer efficient dye degradation but are often limited by secondary pollution and poor effluent reusability. Here, a polydopamine-coated Fe@MXene (PDA@Fe@MXene) composite was developed to overcome these challenges and achieve efficient degradation and resource recovery. The PDA coating increased the specific surface area from 30.4 to 100.2 m2/g and ensured homogeneous elemental dispersion. Under optimal conditions (200 mg/L catalyst, 3 g/L SPS, 0.05 g/L RB5), 99.05% degradation percentage was achieved within 30 min, accompanied by a TOC removal efficiency of 98.1%, indicating effective mineralization. Quenching experiments and EPR analyses revealed the coexistence of radical (‧OH and SO4‧-) and non-radical pathways, with the degradation process showing much higher sensitivity to singlet oxygen (1O2) quenching, while ‧OH and SO4‧- contributed synergistically. The catalyst maintained 90.90% removal efficiency after five cycles and retained 78.86% degradation efficiency after eight consecutive cycles, with low and stabilized Fe leaching (≤0.95 mg/L). Moreover, the treated effluent demonstrated excellent reusability for dyeing, achieving 66.12% dye uptake and high color uniformity. This work provides a practical strategy for the sustainable treatment and reuse of dyeing wastewater, highlighting PDA@Fe@MXene as a stable and eco-efficient catalyst for cleaner textile production.

If there are other authors, they declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.
